Defendant Ric-Wil, Inc.'S Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Aug-25-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Carlos R. Jimenez, Esq. of ARCHER NORRIS (925-930-6600), appearing for defendant / moving party Ric-Wil, Inc.
Christopher Hersom, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Harmon Guy Higgins Jr.

RULING:
Defendant Ric-Wil, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment. Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant's motion is denied. Defendant's alternative motion for summary adjudication is denied as to the first (negligence) and second (products liability) causes of action. A triable issue is created by plaintiff's discovery responses and deposition testimony, defendant's General Order interrogatory responses and the deposition of Mr. Zeile, defendant's person most knowledgeable, whether plaintiff was exposed to an asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant. Defendant's motion for summary adjudication is granted as to the third (false representation), eighteenth (concert of action), and nineteenth and twentieth (fraud, deceit/negligent misrepresentation/concealment) causes of action and as to punitive damages. Plaintiff did not oppose these issues. Defendant's objections to plaintiff's declaration, plaintiff's deposition, defendant's General Order interrogatory responses, the deposition of Mr. Zeile and plaintiff's responses to defendant's special interrogatories are overruled. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 =(220/HEK)
